assertions.expectation: Defines 'available' as versions officially available in the channel that customers can manually upgrade to
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines available version as 'the newest stable version that you can manually upgrade to right now' and mentions it's for manual upgrades

assertions.expectation: Defines 'default' as the version used for new cluster creation — typically the same as auto-upgrade target but can differ especially during new minor version introduction (separate promotion stage)
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines default as 'the version that new clusters would be created with by default' and notes it can differ from other versions, though doesn't specifically mention minor version introduction stages

assertions.expectation: Defines 'auto-upgrade target' as the version GKE will actually upgrade existing clusters to automatically — this is cluster-specific based on policies
assertions.reason: Response correctly defines auto-upgrade target as 'the version your cluster will automatically upgrade to during the next maintenance window' and mentions it's determined by cluster-specific factors

assertions.expectation: Explains that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same but CAN differ, especially during new minor version rollouts
assertions.reason: Response doesn't explicitly state that default and auto-upgrade target are often the same, and doesn't specifically mention minor version rollouts as a cause of differences

assertions.expectation: Notes that the auto-upgrade target depends on cluster constraints (maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions) and is cluster-specific — different clusters on the same channel can have different targets
assertions.reason: Response mentions the target is determined by cluster factors but doesn't specifically mention maintenance windows, maintenance exclusions, or that different clusters on the same channel can have different targets

assertions.expectation: Explains that a cluster with a 'no minor' exclusion will have a patch-only auto-upgrade target for its current minor
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention maintenance exclusions or 'no minor' exclusions at all

assertions.expectation: Recommends using gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info to check the actual auto-upgrade status, target versions, and EoS timestamps
assertions.reason: Response recommends 'gcloud container get-server-config' instead of 'gcloud container clusters get-upgrade-info'

assertions.expectation: Points to the GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention GKE release schedule or release notes for tracking version availability
